text: In these dimly lit, futuristic rooms, I feel a sense of anticipation—perhaps even displacement. Surrounded by glowing screens and cryptic messages about research and openness, I stand at the threshold between the familiar and the unknown. The lighting is otherworldly, almost artificial, which makes me keenly aware of how environments sculpt experience. I am reminded how easily human curiosity is piqued when confronted with mystery. Here, my mind feels both alert and unanchored, aware of possibilities yet to be articulated.
